codeReview.summary: Implementation matches the feature intent: artifact assertions were moved away from page-global text matching into scoped card/pane locators, and behavior coverage across artifact render, approve/deny, and persistence scenarios is preserved.

issues.description: New helper locators rely on Tailwind class-name selectors (user bubble, assistant message, artifact pane). This still improves over page-global text checks, but these selectors are style-coupled and can break on cosmetic class refactors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: playwright-worker skill procedure is broader than this follow-up feature reality: it prescribes full `npx playwright test` and evidence artifact paths in handoff, while the feature contract used targeted grep-scoped Playwright execution and did not include artifact path reporting. Skill guidance may need a clause for assertion-only follow-up features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/playwright-worker/SKILL.md:32-36 requires full run + artifact-path capture; handoff 2026-03-03T16-31-06-908Z__playwright-artifact-locator-hardening__79781bf6-2d27-4e4c-a3ff-e85dfd9a07f1.json lists only command outcomes and no artifact paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no documented stable selector contract for Playwright in AGENTS/library, so tests continue to depend on CSS-class-based locators for message/pane scoping.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/playwright/artifact-approval.e2e.ts:135-148 uses class-substring locators; app/components/ChatMessage.tsx and app/components/artifact-panel/ArtifactPanel.tsx expose class names but no dedicated test IDs for these containers.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit 884a93f for playwright-artifact-locator-hardening. The feature achieves scoped artifact/card assertions with preserved flow coverage and no blocking defects; noted one non-blocking selector-coupling concern and two shared-state observations.
